tool.name: SAM3 Semantic Segmentation
tool.description: SAM3 performs text-prompted semantic segmentation on images or videos.
tool.command:
mkdir data_files && #set $name_file = '' #if $input.input_kind == "image" #for $indata in $input.source ln -s '$indata' 'data_files/${indata.element_identifier}' && #set $name_file += $indata.element_identifier + " " #end for #else ln -s '$input.source' 'data_files/${input.source.element_identifier}' && #set $name_file = $input.source.element_identifier + " " #end if python '$__tool_directory__/sam3_semantic_segmentation.py' --model '$sam3_models.fields.path'
--prompts '$text_prompt' --conf '$conf' --vid_stride '$vid_stride' --outdir outputs --name_file '$name_file' #if $input.input_kind == "video" --quality '$input.quality' #end if #if $input.input_kind == "image" --outputs $input.outputs_format #else #if $input.coco_video_mode != "no_coco" --outputs 'coco, $input.outputs_format' --coco_video_mode '$input.coco_video_mode' #else --outputs $input.outputs_format #end if #end if --do_normalization '$do_normalization' --show_bbox '$show_bbox'

param.name: sam3_models
param.label: Model data
param.help: Contact the administrator of our Galaxy instance if you miss model data

validator.message: No model annotation is available for SAM3

param.name: input_kind
param.label: Input type
param.help: Choose either multiple images or a single video (not both)

validator.message: TIFF images must contain exactly 3 channels (RGB).

param.name: outputs_format
param.label: Output formats
param.help: Select one or more annotation formats to generate.

param.name: quality
param.label: Video quality
param.help: Select output video bitrate,does not affect processing speed or annotations. Higher quality than the original is not useful and will only increase file size.

param.name: coco_video_mode
param.label: COCO output mode
param.help: Controls whether COCO annotations are generated, and how frames are referenced.
param.option: Annotate the video — one COCO entry per frame, referencing the video file
Annotate extracted frames — saves JPGs and one COCO entry per frame image

param.name: outputs_format
param.label: Additional output formats
param.help: YOLO formats are optional. COCO output is controlled separately above.

param.name: text_prompt
param.label: Text prompt
param.help:
Comma-separated list of objects or classes to detect and segment in your images/videos.
Example for Wildlife: elephant, giraffe, lion
Best practices: Use clear, specific terms in English. Be consistent with singular/plural forms.

param.name: vid_stride
param.label: Video frame stride
param.help: For video input: process one frame every N frames.

param.name: show_bbox
param.label: Show bounding boxes on annotated output

param.name: do_normalization
param.label: Normalize outputs?
param.help:
This option will be applied to all selected formats above.
Example: (values scaled between 0 and 1 relative to the image size)
   - Non-normalized bounding box: x=120, y=80, width=50, height=40 (pixel coordinates)
   - Normalized bounding box: x=0.375, y=0.25, width=0.156, height=0.125

data.name: Annotations_coco
data.label: Annotation COCO
data.filter: input['outputs_format'] and 'coco' in input['outputs_format'] or input.get('coco_video_mode') == "video" or input.get('coco_video_mode') == "frames"

tool.help:
=================================================== SAM3 – Text-Prompted Semantic Segmentation ===================================================
SAM3 allows semantic segmentation of objects in images and videos using natural language prompts.
Typical use cases include: - Wildlife or object segmentation in videos - Biomedical image segmentation (cells, tissues) - Environmental or ecological video analysis
------------------- How it works -------------------
1. Provide an image or video 2. Enter a text prompt describing the object to segment 3. SAM3 generates segmentation masks and overlays 4. Results are returned as images or videos
------------------- Notes -------------------
- For large videos, increase the frame stride to reduce processing time
